metadata.archetype_justification: DynamoDB table provides persistent state; POST /books creates records and GET /books reads them. The service owns its data and exposes CRUD operations on book entities.
metadata.context: Serverless REST API with CDK infrastructure for book catalog management. Clean API surface the agent can use as a tool for product lookups.

top_gaps.title: Application Security Pipeline
top_gaps.gap: No SAST, dependency scanning, or security gates in CI/CD pipeline
top_gaps.impact: Vulnerabilities in dependencies or code reach production undetected

top_gaps.title: Backup and Recovery
top_gaps.gap: No PITR or backup configuration for DynamoDB table
top_gaps.impact: Data loss event has no recovery path

top_gaps.title: API Versioning Strategy
top_gaps.gap: No API versioning on any endpoint
top_gaps.impact: Breaking changes affect all consumers simultaneously

top_gaps.title: Business Metrics
top_gaps.impact: Cannot measure whether system delivers business value

top_gaps.title: Incident Response Automation
top_gaps.gap: No runbooks or automated incident response
top_gaps.impact: Incident resolution depends entirely on ad hoc human action

findings.title: Network Security — No VPC endpoints or WAF
findings.description: Lambda functions run in AWS-managed network space. API Gateway provides entry with Cognito auth. DynamoDB access controlled via IAM. No VPC endpoints for DynamoDB, no WAF on API Gateway.
findings.gap: No VPC endpoints for DynamoDB access; no WAF configured on API Gateway.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding AWS WAF on API Gateway for rate limiting and IP-based controls. For sensitive data, place Lambda in VPC with VPC endpoints.

findings.title: API Entry Point — Missing request validation and explicit throttling
findings.description: API Gateway with Cognito auth, CloudWatch logging, and X-Ray tracing. Default throttling applies but no explicit configuration.
findings.gap: No explicit throttling configuration. No request validation models to reject malformed requests before reaching Lambda.
findings.recommendation: Add API Gateway request validators and models. Configure per-method throttling appropriate to expected load.

findings.title: Auto-Scaling — DynamoDB static provisioned capacity
findings.description: Lambda auto-scales inherently. DynamoDB SimpleTable uses default provisioned capacity (5 RCU/5 WCU) with no auto-scaling or on-demand billing configured.
findings.gap: DynamoDB table has static provisioned capacity with no auto-scaling. Will throttle under load.
findings.recommendation: Switch DynamoDB to on-demand billing mode (BillingMode: PAY_PER_REQUEST) or configure Application Auto Scaling.

findings.title: Backup and Recovery — No PITR or backup plan
findings.description: No backup or point-in-time recovery configured for DynamoDB table. No AWS Backup plan.
findings.gap: Data loss event has no recovery path. DynamoDB PITR not enabled.
findings.recommendation: Enable DynamoDB PITR. Add PointInTimeRecoverySpecification: PointInTimeRecoveryEnabled: true to table definition.

findings.title: Programming Languages — AWS SDK v2 in maintenance mode
findings.description: TypeScript 5.7 on Node.js 22.x (modern). Uses AWS SDK v2 (aws-sdk ^2.1692.0) instead of v3.
findings.gap: AWS SDK v2 is maintenance-mode. Larger bundle sizes, no modular imports, missing middleware stack.
findings.recommendation: Migrate to @aws-sdk/client-dynamodb and @aws-sdk/lib-dynamodb (v3) for smaller bundles and better TypeScript support.

findings.title: API Versioning Strategy — No versioning
findings.description: No API versioning strategy. Endpoints are /books with no version prefix or headers.
findings.gap: Breaking changes affect all consumers simultaneously with no migration path.
findings.recommendation: Adopt URL-path versioning (/v1/books). Define strategy before adding new endpoints or modifying schema.

findings.title: Service Discovery — Environment variables only, no API catalog
findings.description: Environment variables for DynamoDB table name and Lambda ARN. No dynamic service discovery or API catalog.
findings.gap: No service registry or API catalog to make this service discoverable by other services or agents.
findings.recommendation: Publish a standalone OpenAPI specification. Register API in a service catalog for discoverability.

findings.title: Unified Data Access Layer — DynamoDB access duplicated inline
findings.description: Each Lambda handler directly instantiates DynamoDB client and executes queries. No shared repository/DAO layer.
findings.gap: Client instantiation and query logic duplicated across handlers. Schema changes require updates in every handler.
findings.recommendation: Extract a shared books-repository module encapsulating DynamoDB client creation and CRUD operations.

findings.title: Encryption at Rest — AWS-managed keys only
findings.description: DynamoDB SSE enabled with AWS-owned keys. S3 buckets use S3_MANAGED encryption. All data stores encrypted.
findings.gap: Using AWS-managed/owned keys rather than customer-managed KMS keys. No explicit key rotation policy.
findings.recommendation: For public book catalog, AWS-managed encryption is sufficient. Migrate to CMK if data sensitivity increases.

findings.title: API Authentication — Public GET endpoint
findings.description: POST /books requires Cognito OAuth2 auth. GET /books is intentionally public for book catalog access.
findings.gap: GET /books lacks explicit rate limiting or API key requirement. Public endpoint could be abused.
findings.recommendation: Add API Gateway usage plans with throttling on public GET endpoint for abuse protection.

findings.title: Centralized Identity — No external IdP federation
findings.description: Cognito User Pool with OAuth2 flows. Only COGNITO as supported identity provider.
findings.gap: No federation with external IdPs (Okta, Azure AD, SAML). SSO not enabled.
findings.recommendation: Add SAML/OIDC federation if organizational SSO is needed. Cognito-only acceptable for standalone API.

findings.title: Compute Hardening — No dependency vulnerability scanning
findings.description: Lambda managed runtime (nodejs22.x) — AWS handles OS patching. No dependency vulnerability scanning configured.
findings.gap: Application dependencies not scanned for known vulnerabilities.
findings.recommendation: Add npm audit to build phase. Integrate Snyk or AWS Inspector for Lambda function scanning.

findings.title: Application Security Pipeline — No security scanning
findings.description: No SAST, DAST, dependency scanning, or container scanning in CI/CD pipeline. Only unit tests run.
findings.gap: Vulnerabilities in dependencies or source code reach production without automated security validation.
findings.recommendation: Add Dependabot or npm audit --audit-level=high. Integrate Semgrep or CodeGuru Reviewer for SAST.

findings.title: SLO Definitions — Only basic error alarms
findings.description: CloudWatch Alarms for Lambda Errors > 0. No formal SLOs, no latency alarms, no error budgets.
findings.gap: No formal SLOs defined. No latency-based alarms or error budget tracking.
findings.recommendation: Define SLOs: availability 99.9%, latency p99 < 500ms. Add CloudWatch alarms on API Gateway latency.

findings.title: Business Metrics — No custom metrics published
findings.description: No CloudWatch putMetricData calls. Only default Lambda/API Gateway infrastructure metrics available.
findings.gap: Cannot measure business outcomes: books created, queries performed, API adoption rate.
findings.recommendation: Add CloudWatch Embedded Metric Format (EMF) for BooksCreated, BooksQueried counts in Lambda handlers.

findings.title: Anomaly Detection — Static thresholds only
findings.description: Static threshold alarms (Errors > 0). No anomaly detection, no composite alarms, no latency alerting.
findings.gap: Static thresholds miss gradual degradation and novel failure patterns.
findings.recommendation: Add CloudWatch Anomaly Detection on latency and duration metrics. Add composite alarms.

findings.title: Incident Response Automation — No runbooks
findings.description: No runbooks, no SSM Automation documents, no automated incident response workflows.
findings.gap: Incident response entirely ad hoc. No documented procedures for common failures.
findings.recommendation: Create runbooks for DynamoDB throttling, Lambda error spikes, API Gateway 5xx. Start with markdown, evolve to SSM Automation.

findings.title: Observability Ownership — No ownership attribution
findings.description: No CODEOWNERS file. Alarms have no team attribution. No dashboards in IaC.
findings.gap: No clear ownership of observability assets. No defined escalation path.
findings.recommendation: Add CODEOWNERS file. Add owner tags to alarms. Define CloudWatch dashboard in SAM template.

findings.title: Resource Tagging — Minimal tags, placeholder values
findings.description: Basic tags: project: my-project, environment: Stage. Only on Lambda and DynamoDB. Missing on Cognito, IAM, Alarms.
findings.gap: Only 2 tag keys with placeholder values. Missing owner, cost-center, team. No enforcement. Incomplete coverage.
findings.recommendation: Define tagging standard with required keys. Apply via SAM Globals. Replace placeholder values. Add Config required-tags rule.

evaluations.title: Managed Compute
evaluations.reason: All compute on Lambda (serverless). No EC2.

evaluations.title: Managed Databases
evaluations.reason: DynamoDB fully managed with SSE enabled.

evaluations.title: Workflow Orchestration
evaluations.reason: No multi-step business workflows exist. Simple single-step CRUD operations.

evaluations.title: Async Messaging and Streaming
evaluations.reason: Self-contained service with no cross-service state propagation. Sync is correct design.

evaluations.title: High Availability and Fault Isolation
evaluations.reason: All managed services inherently multi-AZ (Lambda, DynamoDB, API Gateway).

evaluations.title: Infrastructure as Code Coverage
evaluations.reason: 100% IaC via SAM + CDK. All resources defined in code.

evaluations.title: Monolith vs Microservices
evaluations.reason: Serverless microservice with independent Lambda functions and dedicated DynamoDB table.

evaluations.title: Async vs Sync Communication
evaluations.reason: Self-contained service with no inter-service communication. Sync is correct.

evaluations.title: Long-Running Process Handling
evaluations.reason: All operations complete in milliseconds. Lambda timeout 5s. No long-running operations.

evaluations.title: Unstructured Data Storage
evaluations.reason: Service handles only structured data (book records). No unstructured data use case.

evaluations.title: Database Engine Version and EOL
evaluations.reason: DynamoDB is serverless — no engine version or EOL concern.

evaluations.title: Stored Procedures and Schema Complexity
evaluations.reason: DynamoDB has no stored procedures. All logic in application layer.

evaluations.title: Audit Logging
evaluations.reason: Application-level IaC only. CloudTrail is account-level, belongs in foundation repo.

evaluations.title: Secrets Management
evaluations.reason: Fully IAM-based. No secrets to manage. No hardcoded credentials.

evaluations.title: Distributed Tracing
evaluations.reason: X-Ray fully instrumented: Lambda Active, API Gateway TracingEnabled, SDK captured.

evaluations.title: Deployment Strategy
evaluations.reason: Linear10PercentEvery1Minute with alarms and pre-traffic hooks.

evaluations.title: Integration Testing
evaluations.reason: Comprehensive e2e tests in CI pipeline against staging environment.

pathways.name: Move to Containers

triggering_questions.note: INF-Q1 = 4; compute is Lambda, not EC2

pathways.not_triggered_reason: Compute is already Lambda/serverless. Contextual guard prevents trigger — SHALL NOT trigger if compute is already Lambda/Fargate/ECS.

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: DATA-Q4 = 4 — no stored procedures or proprietary SQL; DynamoDB is not a commercial DB

triggering_questions.note: No proprietary SQL or stored procedures
DynamoDB is already a fully managed database service

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q4 = 4; no data processing workloads exist

triggering_questions.note: No streaming/analytics infrastructure needed; contextual guard blocks trigger

pathways.not_triggered_reason: No evidence of data processing workloads. This is a simple CRUD API with no streaming, ETL, or analytics requirements.

pathways.name: Move to Modern DevOps
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q10 = 4, INF-Q11 = 4 — full IaC and CI/CD already in place

triggering_questions.note: Full IaC coverage via SAM + CDK
Full CI/CD pipeline with automated rollback

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context. Context mentions 'agent' but not in the AI signal terms list.
